title: Operator TUI Markdown and Mermaid rendering in center VisualViewport
purpose: Add a dedicated center viewport view that can render Markdown documents and Mermaid diagrams inside the Operator TUI using the existing VisualViewport renderer/adapter architecture. Markdown must work with a pure ANSI fallback. Mermaid diagrams should render as SVG/PNG frames when supported and fall back to visible source blocks with clear error reasons when rendering is unavailable.
goal: Implement a markdown_mermaid_document_view that can display Markdown and Mermaid content in the middle TUI area, reuse the existing view/renderer/output-adapter contracts, support runtime switching, cache rendered diagrams, and expose capability status to the view switcher overlay.

context.user_goal: Markdown und Mermaid sollen in der mittleren TUI einfach renderbar sein.
context.design_decision: Markdown is rendered directly to ANSI/Rich-style text first. Mermaid rendering is optional and should use mermaid-cli or a headless browser/Playwright path to produce SVG/PNG, then the existing raster/output adapters display it. If unavailable, the original Mermaid code block is shown with a concise reason.

rendering_modes.markdown_ansi: Markdown -> parsed blocks -> ANSI/text frame -> CenterViewport
rendering_modes.markdown_raster_optional: Markdown -> HTML/SVG/PNG preview -> raster frame -> Sixel/Kitty/ANSI fallback
rendering_modes.mermaid_image: Mermaid source -> SVG/PNG via renderer backend -> raster frame -> Sixel/Kitty
rendering_modes.mermaid_fallback: Mermaid source -> fenced code block with error/capability reason -> ANSI

recommended_defaults.safe_default: never execute arbitrary shell commands except configured trusted renderer binaries

security_rules: Do not execute Mermaid/Markdown embedded scripts.
Do not load remote resources from Markdown/HTML/Mermaid by default.
Only render files from allowed project/artifact/document roots.
Mermaid rendering must run with timeout and size limits.
Renderer command paths must be configured/trusted, not taken from document content.
Fallback must show source safely if rendering fails.

milestones.title: Markdown/Mermaid view contract and safe configuration
Center viewport integration and view switcher capability reporting

tasks.title: Define markdown_mermaid_document view contract
tasks.description: Document the Markdown/Mermaid view as a normal VisualView running inside the center VisualViewport.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation contains a Mermaid flowchart for Markdown and Mermaid render paths.
Documentation states that the view never writes terminal escape sequences directly.
Documentation defines fallback behavior for unsupported Mermaid rendering.
Documentation explains how the view appears in the two-line view switcher overlay as available or unavailable.

tasks.title: Add Markdown/Mermaid config model
tasks.description: Add typed/defaulted config for Markdown rendering mode, Mermaid renderer selection, cache behavior, timeouts and allowed roots.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports markdown_mode values ansi, raster_optional and source_only.
Config supports mermaid_mode values auto, image, source_only and disabled.
Config supports mermaid_renderers order with mermaid_cli, playwright and fallback_codeblock.
Config supports timeout_seconds, max_diagram_width, max_diagram_height, cache_enabled and allowed_roots.
Unsafe or unknown config values fail with clear diagnostics.

tasks.title: Add safe document source descriptor
tasks.description: Represent Markdown sources safely without allowing arbitrary file access from the TUI view.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentSource supports inline text, artifact reference and allowed file path reference.
File sources are rejected when outside configured allowed roots.
Missing or unreadable documents produce a user-visible placeholder frame, not a crash.
Source descriptor contains stable identity/hash data for cache keys.

tasks.title: Implement Markdown block parser
tasks.description: Parse Markdown into a small internal block model suitable for ANSI rendering and Mermaid block extraction.
acceptance_criteria: Parser recognizes headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, fenced code blocks, tables as fallback text, blockquotes and horizontal rules.
Parser detects fenced mermaid code blocks without losing their original source.
Parser is deterministic and does not execute embedded HTML/scripts.
Invalid Markdown still produces readable fallback blocks.

tasks.title: Implement Markdown ANSI renderer
tasks.description: Render parsed Markdown blocks to ANSI/text frames for the center viewport fallback path.
acceptance_criteria: Headings, lists, paragraphs, code fences and blockquotes are visibly distinct in ANSI/text mode.
Output is clipped to the assigned viewport width and height.
Long documents support scroll offset or paging state.
Rendering works without optional image or Mermaid dependencies.
Snapshot tests can assert stable output for fixed viewport sizes.

tasks.title: Add syntax-highlight placeholder strategy
tasks.description: Provide simple readable code block rendering first; optional syntax highlighting can be added later without changing the view contract.
acceptance_criteria: Code blocks display language label when present.
Code blocks are framed or indented consistently.
No dependency on pygments or rich is required for the base path.
Optional syntax highlighting is explicitly documented as a future enhancement, not required for MVP.

tasks.title: Implement Mermaid block extractor
tasks.description: Extract Mermaid fenced blocks from parsed Markdown and assign deterministic block IDs/hashes.
acceptance_criteria: Extractor recognizes fenced code blocks labeled mermaid.
Extractor preserves exact Mermaid source for rendering and fallback display.
Each block receives a stable hash based on source and render config.
Multiple Mermaid blocks in one document are supported.

tasks.title: Implement Mermaid renderer backend abstraction
tasks.description: Create a safe abstraction for Mermaid-to-SVG/PNG rendering through configured renderer backends.
acceptance_criteria: MermaidRenderer has backends for mermaid_cli, playwright and fallback_codeblock or equivalent abstractions.
Renderer reports availability without crashing when node, mmdc or browser dependencies are missing.
Renderer runs with timeout, max output size and clear error reporting.
Renderer never loads remote resources by default.
Renderer returns raster/SVG frame payloads compatible with existing VisualViewport render/output adapters.

tasks.title: Implement Mermaid render cache
tasks.description: Cache rendered Mermaid diagrams by source hash, renderer backend and target size.
acceptance_criteria: Cache key includes Mermaid source hash, renderer backend, target format and size/theme options.
Cache can be disabled by config.
Cache failures do not prevent fallback rendering.
Diagnostics expose cache hit/miss and render duration.

tasks.title: Implement Mermaid fallback code block rendering
tasks.description: Show Mermaid source safely when image rendering is unavailable or fails.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback displays a concise reason such as 'Mermaid image renderer unavailable: mmdc not found'.
Fallback displays the original Mermaid source as a fenced code block.
Fallback is readable in the center viewport ANSI mode.
A failed Mermaid block does not prevent the rest of the Markdown document from rendering.

tasks.title: Implement markdown_mermaid_document_view
tasks.description: Add a VisualView implementation that renders Markdown documents and embedded Mermaid diagrams inside the center viewport.
acceptance_criteria: View implements the existing VisualView contract.
View supports inline Markdown text and DocumentSource references.
View can render in pure ANSI mode without Mermaid image support.
View can include Mermaid image frames when a Mermaid renderer and image-capable output adapter are available.
View exposes metadata and requirements for the view switcher overlay.

tasks.title: Register Markdown/Mermaid view in VisualViewport registry
tasks.description: Make the new view selectable like logo, snake, artifact preview and diagnostics views.
acceptance_criteria: View id markdown_mermaid_document is registered by default.
View can be selected through the same switching mechanism as other center views.
If only ANSI is available, the view still appears under Views OK because Markdown text fallback works.
If Mermaid image rendering is unavailable, the view remains available but reports Mermaid image mode as degraded.

tasks.title: Integrate Markdown/Mermaid capability reporting with view switcher overlay
tasks.description: Expose whether Markdown and Mermaid rendering are fully available, degraded or unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay can show markdown_mermaid_document under Views OK when ANSI Markdown fallback is available.
Overlay detail/diagnostics reports Mermaid status: image, degraded/source-only or disabled.
Unavailable reasons are concise and actionable, for example 'Mermaid image: mmdc missing'.
The active view marker works for markdown_mermaid_document.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for Markdown parsing and ANSI rendering
tasks.description: Verify deterministic Markdown parsing/rendering without optional dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover headings, paragraphs, lists, code blocks, tables as fallback text and blockquotes.
Tests cover viewport clipping and scroll offset/paging behavior.
Tests run without Mermaid, node, browser or image protocol support.
Snapshot output is stable for fixed viewport sizes.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for Mermaid extraction, rendering availability and fallback
tasks.description: Verify Mermaid handling works with and without external renderer support.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover one and multiple Mermaid blocks in a Markdown document.
Tests cover renderer unavailable producing source-code fallback with reason.
Tests cover timeout/error path producing fallback, not a crash.
Tests use fake renderer backends rather than requiring real mmdc/playwright in CI.

tasks.title: Add manual smoke command for Markdown/Mermaid center rendering
tasks.description: Provide a quick manual tool to render a Markdown/Mermaid sample through ANSI and optional image paths.
acceptance_criteria: Script accepts a Markdown file path and a mode flag.
Script prints Mermaid renderer availability diagnostics.
Script can render ANSI fallback output without starting full Operator TUI.
Script can optionally emit SVG/PNG/Sixel/Kitty test output when supported.

tasks.title: Document usage examples and failure modes
tasks.description: Add practical examples for using the center Markdown/Mermaid view with project docs and generated agent artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include a minimal Markdown example and a Mermaid diagram example.
Docs explain how to switch to the view from the ViewSwitcher overlay.
Docs explain dependency choices for mermaid-cli and Playwright paths.
Docs list common failures: mmdc missing, browser missing, timeout, unsupported image protocol, and source-only fallback.
Docs state that Markdown remains usable even when Mermaid image rendering is unavailable.

summary_notes: Markdown should be usable immediately through ANSI/text fallback once the view exists.
Mermaid image rendering must be optional because it depends on node/mermaid-cli or a browser path.
The center VisualViewport architecture makes this a normal view, not a special TUI hack.
Security defaults must prevent arbitrary shell execution, remote resource loading and file access outside allowed roots.

end_summaries.title: Expected result after completion
items: The Operator TUI can switch to a markdown_mermaid_document center view.
Markdown documents render readably in ANSI/text mode without optional dependencies.
Mermaid blocks render as images when configured renderer support exists.
Mermaid blocks fall back to visible source code with a clear reason when image rendering is unavailable.
The view switcher overlay reports the Markdown/Mermaid view as available, degraded or unavailable based on real capabilities.
